644	/-- **Characterization of `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` by section localization.** For a sheaf of modules
645	`M` on `Spec R`, the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an isomorphism iff for every `f : R` the
646	section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
647	
648	The forward direction is the affine engine `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`; the
649	reverse is `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`. Combined with G1-core
650	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`, not yet
651	formalized) — which supplies the right-hand side for any quasi-coherent `M` — this yields gap1
652	(`lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`). Project-local. -/
653	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
654	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
655	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
656	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
657	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom :=
658	  ⟨fun h f => by haveI := h; exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f,
659	    isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict M⟩
660	
661	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1-core (Route F) building blocks
662	
663	The keystone G1-core `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization` asks: for a *quasi-coherent*
664	`M : (Spec R).Modules` and `f : R`, the section restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is
665	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`. Via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict` this is
666	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` — i.e. to the statement that a quasi-coherent sheaf on an
667	affine scheme lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence). That
668	equivalence is a genuine Mathlib gap at the pinned commit (Tilde.lean only proves the *globally
669	presented* case, `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
670	
671	The composition lemma below discharges the **globally-presented** sub-case end to end (it is the
672	Route-F endpoint once a global presentation/tilde identification is in hand). The residual gap is
673	exactly the production of a global presentation/tilde from local (quasi-coherent) data on `Spec R`;
674	see the handoff in `task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
675	
676	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a globally presented module.** If `M : (Spec R).Modules`
677	admits a global `SheafOfModules.Presentation`, then for every `f : R` the section restriction
678	`Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
679	
680	This is the composition of Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (a global presentation
681	forces `M.fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism, i.e. `M` is a `tilde`) with the affine engine
682	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`. It is the Route-F endpoint for the
683	globally-presented case; the general quasi-coherent case additionally requires producing a global
684	presentation/tilde identification from the (Mathlib-absent) affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` bridge.
685	Project-local. -/
686	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
687	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (_P : M.Presentation) (f : R) :
688	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
689	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
690	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
691	  haveI : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation M _P
692	  exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f
693	
694	/-- **`map_units` field of G1-core (Route F), for any sheaf of modules.** For `M : (Spec R).Modules`
695	and `f : R`, every element of `Submonoid.powers f` acts invertibly on the sections `Γ(M, D(f))` over
696	`R`. This is exactly the first field `IsLocalizedModule.map_units` of the target
697	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, in the shape the 3-field constructor consumes.
698	
699	It holds for an *arbitrary* `M` (no quasi-coherence needed): on `D(f)` the element `f` already maps
700	to a unit of the structure ring `Γ(O_{Spec R}, D(f))` (the away-localization
701	`IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`), and the `R`-action on `Γ(M, D(f))` factors through it via
702	the scalar tower `R → Γ(O, D(f)) → Γ(M, D(f))`. Packaged from Mathlib's
703	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen`. Project-local only as the *named* field
704	of the Route-F decomposition; the substance of G1-core is `surj`/`exists_of_eq` (see handoff). -/
705	theorem map_units_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R) :
706	    ∀ x : Submonoid.powers f, IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R
707	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj
708	        (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) (x : R)) := by
709	  rintro ⟨x, n, rfl⟩
710	  simpa using (tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M f).pow n
711	
712	/-- **Finite basic-open cover refining a quasi-coherent presentation cover.** Given a
713	sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` together with quasi-coherent data `q` (a — possibly
714	infinite — open cover `q.X : q.I → (Spec R).Opens` of `⊤` with a presentation of
715	`M.over (q.X i)` on each member), there is a *finite* family of elements `t : Finset R`
716	whose basic opens cover `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`), with each basic open `D(r)`
717	(`r ∈ t`) contained in some member `q.X i` of the presentation cover.
718	
719	This is the topological "finite-cover front" of `lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover`:
720	quasi-compactness of `Spec R` plus the basic-open basis (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`)
721	refine `q.X` to a finite basic-open subcover; the cover condition `q.coversTop` is read off the
722	`Opens.grothendieckTopology` sieve via `Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff`, and finiteness is extracted
723	through `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. To obtain `q` from `[M.IsQuasicoherent]`, take
724	`‹M.IsQuasicoherent›.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some`.
725	
726	The remaining (heavy) step toward gap1 — transporting each presentation `q.presentation i`
727	of `M.over (q.X i)` across `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` to `IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)` — is the
728	site-slice ↔ scheme-pullback transport, which has no Mathlib support at the pinned commit.
729	Project-local: Mathlib has no affine quasi-coherent → finite presentation cover lemma. -/
730	theorem exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
731	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
732	    ∃ t : Finset R, Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤ ∧
733	      ∀ r ∈ t, ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i := by
734	  classical
735	  set G : Set R := {r | ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i} with hG
736	  have hspanG : Ideal.span G = ⊤ := by
737	    rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff']
738	    rw [eq_top_iff]
739	    intro x _
740	    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
741	    obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hxU⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ x (by trivial)
742	    rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hf
743	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hUi⟩⟩ := hf
744	    have hxXi : x ∈ q.X i := (leOfHom hUi) hxU
745	    obtain ⟨V, ⟨r, rfl⟩, hxV, hVle⟩ :=
746	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens) hxXi
747	    exact ⟨r, ⟨i, hVle⟩, hxV⟩
748	  obtain ⟨t, htsub, htspan⟩ := (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite G).mp hspanG
749	  exact ⟨t, htspan, fun r hr => htsub hr⟩
750	
751	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence
752	
753	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
754	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
755	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
756	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
757	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
758	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
759	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
760	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
761	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
762	
763	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
764	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
765	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
766	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
767	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
768	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
769	
770	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
771	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
772	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
773	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
774	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
775	
776	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
777	
778	open TopologicalSpace Topology
779	
780	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
781	
782	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
783	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
784	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
785	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
786	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
787	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
788	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
789	  cover_lift := by
790	    intro Y S hS
791	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
792	    intro x hx
793	    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
794	    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
795	    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
796	    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
797	    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
798	      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
799	    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
800	      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
801	    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
802	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
803	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
804	    have hdomle :
805	        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
806	      intro z hz
807	      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
808	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
809	    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1
810	
811	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
812	Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
813	topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
814	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
815	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
816	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
817	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
818	  cover_lift := by
819	    intro W S hS
820	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
821	    intro y hy
822	    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
823	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
824	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
825	    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
826	    set V : Opens ↥U :=
827	      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
828	    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
829	      intro z hz
830	      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
831	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
832	    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
833	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
834	    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
835	      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
836	    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
837	    · apply Subsingleton.elim
838	
839	/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
840	cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
841	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
842	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
843	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
844	  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
845	
846	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
847	inverse and the equivalence adjunction `inverse ⊣ functor`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
848	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
849	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous :
850	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
851	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) := by
852	  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.functor.IsCocontinuous
853	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
854	    inferInstanceAs ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous _ _)
855	  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
856	
857	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
858	functor and the equivalence adjunction `functor ⊣ inverse`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
859	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
860	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous :
861	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
862	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
863	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
864	
865	/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
866	`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites
867	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
868	lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
869	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
870	
871	This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
872	foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
873	`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
874	sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
875	transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
876	presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
877	noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
878	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
879	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
880	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
881	
882	end OverSiteSheafEquivalence

884	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice-to-geometric module bridge (gap1, C)
885	
886	This section builds the geometric layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge
887	`lem:over_restrict_iso` on top of the topological `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` of the previous
888	section. It identifies, on the level of *sheaves of modules*, the abstract Grothendieck-slice
889	restriction `M.over U` (a sheaf of modules over the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice
890	site `J.over U`) with the geometric restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M` on the small Zariski
891	site of the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
892	
893	The key structural facts, all holding *definitionally* at the pinned commit, are:
894	* `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` (`rfl`): the inverse leg
895	  of the site equivalence, post-composed with the slice-forgetful functor, is the opens functor of
896	  the open immersion `U.ι`;
897	* consequently `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
898	  (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (`rfl`): the structure sheaf of the open subscheme is the transport of
899	  the sliced structure sheaf across the ring-valued sheaf equivalence (this is *step 2*, the
900	  geometric ring-sheaf identification, discharged by `rfl`).
901	
902	From these, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` lifts the site equivalence to an
903	equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules (`overRestrictEquiv`, *step 3*), under whose functor
904	the sliced module `M.over U` corresponds to the geometric restriction (`overRestrictIso`, *step 4*).
905	
906	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies only the underlying site equivalence and the
907	`pushforward`/`restrictFunctor` machinery; the assembly is project-local. -/
908	
909	section OverRestrictBridge
910	
911	open TopologicalSpace Topology
912	
913	namespace Scheme.Modules
914	
915	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)
916	
917	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules** (gap1, C, step 3).
918	
919	For a scheme `X` and an open `U ⊆ X`, the category of sheaves of modules over the sliced structure
920	sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice site `J.over U` is equivalent to the category `U.toScheme.Modules`
921	of sheaves of modules on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The equivalence is obtained by lifting the
922	topological site equivalence `Opens.overEquivalence U` (and its ring-valued sheaf congruence
923	`overEquivalence_sheafCongr`) to sheaves of modules via
924	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`; the two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms it
925	consumes are the (co)unit of `Opens.overEquivalence U` whiskered into the structure presheaf, and
926	the identity (the geometric structure sheaf being *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one).
927	
928	Project-local: it is the module-level layer of the gap1 bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso`; Mathlib
929	supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
930	noncomputable def overRestrictEquiv :
931	    SheafOfModules.{u} (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules :=
932	  letI eqv := Opens.overEquivalence U
933	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
934	      (J := (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (K := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) eqv
935	    (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
936	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (X.ringCatSheaf.over U).obj))
937	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (𝟙 _))
938	    (by ext : 2
939	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
940	          Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app, NatTrans.id_app,
941	          ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app]
942	        exact congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map
943	          (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm))
944	    (by ext : 2
945	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
946	          NatTrans.op_app, ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.id_app,
947	          Functor.comp_obj]
948	        erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
949	        rename_i x
950	        have h : (eqv.unitIso.inv.app (Opposite.unop x)).op ≫ (eqv.unit.app (Opposite.unop x)).op
951	            = 𝟙 _ := by
952	          rw [← op_comp]
953	          simp
954	        exact (congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map h).trans
955	          (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _))).symm
956	
957	/-- **Step-4 functor identification of the gap1 bridge.** The composite of `SheafOfModules.over · U`
958	with the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is the geometric restriction functor
959	`restrictFunctor U.ι` along the open immersion `U.ι`. Both are pushforwards along the immersion's
960	opens functor (`(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`); the
961	two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms agree, so the identification is `pushforwardComp` followed by
962	`pushforwardCongr`. Project-local. -/
963	noncomputable def overRestrictFunctorIso :
964	    (SheafOfModules.pushforward (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (𝟙 _)) ⋙
965	        (overRestrictEquiv U).functor ≅ restrictFunctor U.ι :=
966	  haveI : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).IsContinuous
967	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X) :=
968	    Functor.isContinuous_comp (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse (Over.forget U)
969	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U)
970	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X)
971	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
972	
973	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism on an object** (gap1, C, step 4): for a sheaf of modules
974	`M` on `X`, the transport of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under the
975	slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically isomorphic to the geometric
976	restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M`. This is the object-level form of
977	`overRestrictFunctorIso`; composed with `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` it lands the geometric
978	restriction as the pullback `U.ι^* M`. Project-local: the load-bearing slice-touching ingredient of
979	the gap1 transport `lem:over_restrict_iso`. -/
980	noncomputable def overRestrictIso (M : X.Modules) :
981	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M :=
982	  (overRestrictFunctorIso U).app M
983	
984	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form** (gap1, C, step 4'): the transport of the
985	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically
986	isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) `U.ι^* M` of `M` along the open immersion `U.ι`. This is
987	`overRestrictIso` composed with Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`; it is the form a
988	presentation of `M.over U` transports into a presentation of the geometric pullback `U.ι^* M`.
989	Project-local. -/
990	noncomputable def overRestrictPullbackIso (M : X.Modules) :
991	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M :=
992	  overRestrictIso U M ≪≫ (restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).app M
993	
994	end Scheme.Modules
995	
996	end OverRestrictBridge
997	
998	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice-to-geometric presentation transport (gap1, P1)
999	
1000	This section builds the geometric milestone of the gap1 per-element transport
1001	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1002	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` is transported, across the slice-to-geometric
1003	bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C), into a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the *geometric*
1004	restriction `(Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
1005	
1006	The load-bearing ingredient is the unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso`: the slice-to-geometric
1007	equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
1008	along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the
1009	structure-sheaf module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
1010	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +
1011	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1012	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1013	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is
1014	(here `ψ = 𝟙`).
1015	
1016	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
1017	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1018	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are
1019	project-local. -/
1020	
1021	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1022	
1023	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1024	
1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1033	
1034	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1035	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1036	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1037	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1038	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1039	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1040	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1041	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1042	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1043	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1044	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1045	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1046	  haveI := hψ
1047	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1048	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1049	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1050	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1051	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1052	  intro V
1053	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1054	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1055	  convert this using 1
1056	
1057	namespace Scheme.Modules
1058	
1059	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1060	
1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1080	
1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1091	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1092	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1093	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1094	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1095	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1096	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1097	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1098	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))
1099	
1100	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1101	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1103	/-- **Geometric restriction to a cover member is globally presented** (gap1, P1).
1104	
1105	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q` and an index `i`, the geometric
1106	restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M = (q.X i).ι^* M` of `M` to the open subscheme
1107	`(q.X i).toScheme` admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation`. It is `overRestrictPresentation` applied
1108	to the slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` supplied by the
1109	quasi-coherence datum.
1110	
1111	This is the per-cover-member output that feeds the affine descent of the gap1 transport
1112	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): for `D(r) ≤ q.X i` one further restricts this
1113	presentation to the basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1114	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. The heartbeat headroom tames the slice-site
1115	`IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up that `Presentation.map` triggers across the

3121	  \textit{The one lemma that must touch the Grothendieck slice site; everything downstream of it is
3122	  geometric.}
3123	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(U \subseteq X\) an open. The \emph{abstract Grothendieck-slice}
3124	  restriction \(M.\mathrm{over}\,U\) — an object of the category of sheaves of modules over the
3125	  sliced structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X.\mathrm{over}\,U\) on the slice site \(J.\mathrm{over}\,U\) —
3126	  is canonically isomorphic to the \emph{geometric} restriction

3129	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib}), as a sheaf of modules on the small Zariski
3130	  site of the open subscheme \(U.\mathrm{toScheme}\). The isomorphism is induced by the equivalence
3131	  of sites \(J.\mathrm{over}\,U \simeq \mathrm{Opens}(U.\mathrm{toScheme})\) coming from the opens

3147	  \(U.\iota\) realizes the slice of the opens site of \(X\) over \(U\) as the opens site of the

3321	\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover (gap1, P1)]
3322	  \label{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}
3323	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen}
3324	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3325	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3326	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib,
3327	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}
3328	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen does NOT yet exist; it is the
3329	  % per-element transport step of gap1. It replaces the former lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover,
3330	  % whose \lean{} pin named a non-existent decl (and whose Mayer--Vietoris framing is superseded by
3331	  % the section-localization descent of lem:section_localization_descent).
3332	  \textit{Per-element step: on each basic open of the refined cover, the restricted sheaf is a
3333	  geometric tilde.}
3334	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) with quasi-coherence data \(q\), and let \(r \in R\)
3335	  be such that \(D(r) \le q.X_i\) for some member of the cover (as produced by
3336	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). Then the restricted counit is an
3337	  isomorphism,
3338	  \[
3339	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr),
3340	  \]
3341	  i.e. \(M|_{D(r)}\) is identified with the tilde \(\widetilde{N}\) of the \(R_r\)-module
3342	  \(N = \Gamma(M, D(r))\) on \(\Spec R_r \cong D(r)\).
3343	\end{lemma}
3344	\begin{proof}
3345	  \uses{lem:over_restrict_iso, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso, lem:presentation_map_mathlib,
3346	    lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib, lem:modules_pullback_mathlib,
3347	    lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib,
3348	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData,
3349	    def:over_restrict_presentation, def:presentation_pullback_iota_of_quasicoherentData}
3350	  The quasi-coherence datum supplies a presentation
3351	  \(q.\mathrm{presentation}\,i : (M.\mathrm{over}\,(q.X_i)).\mathrm{Presentation}\) on the slice over
3352	  \(q.X_i\). Restrict it along the inclusion of opens \(D(r) \subseteq q.X_i\) by transporting across
3353	  the iterated Grothendieck slice — the template is \(\mathrm{QuasicoherentData.bind}\)
3354	  (\cref{lem:quasicoherentData_bind_mathlib}) — using \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\)
3355	  (\cref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib}) along the colimit-preserving slice functor; this yields a
3356	  presentation of \(M.\mathrm{over}\,(D(r))\). Pushing through the slice-to-geometric bridge
3357	  \cref{lem:over_restrict_iso} converts it into a presentation of the geometric restriction
3358	  \((\mathrm{restrictFunctor}\,(D(r)).\iota).\mathrm{obj}\,M\); then, across the affine
3359	  identification \(D(r) \cong \Spec R_r\) (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, by
3360	  \(\mathrm{Presentation.map}\) along the pullback functor \cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}), into
3361	  a genuine global presentation of \(M|_{D(r)}\) as a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R_r\). A global
3362	  presentation forces the \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-counit to be an isomorphism
3363	  (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation_mathlib}), giving
3364	  \(\mathrm{IsIso}\bigl((M|_{D(r)}).\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\bigr)\).
3365	\end{proof}

3367	% --- (D) The section-localization descent: the gap1 keystone ---
3368	
3369	\begin{lemma}[Section-localization descent from a local-tilde cover (gap1 keystone, D)]
3370	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent}
3371	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent}
3372	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3373	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3374	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3375	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3376	  % NOTE: the pinned Lean decl isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent does NOT yet exist; it is the
3377	  % genuine new content of gap1 and is reused by gap2's section-localization characterization.
3378	  % isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation does NOT close it: that lemma needs a GLOBAL
3379	  % presentation of M, whereas here only per-D(r) tilde data is available.
3380	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S "Sections over principal opens",
3381	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2152-L2170).
3382	  % Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.3.
3383	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3384	  % "Let $X$ be a scheme. Let $f \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
3385	  %  Denote $X_f \subset X$ the open where $f$ is invertible, see
3386	  %  Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-f-open}.
3387	  %  If $X$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated, the canonical map
3388	  %  $$
3389	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{O}_X)
3390	  %  $$
3391	  %  is an isomorphism. Moreover, if $\mathcal{F}$ is a quasi-coherent
3392	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules the map
3393	  %  $$
3394	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})
3395	  %  $$
3396	  %  is an isomorphism."
3397	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3398	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3399	  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) (equivalently, by
3400	  \cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent} and
3401	  \cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}, \(M\) is locally isomorphic to a
3402	  tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) on the members of a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) of
3403	  \(\Spec R\)). Then for every \(f \in R\) the section restriction
3404	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits the target as the localized module
3405	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); that is, it is
3406	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
3407	\end{lemma}
3408	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
3409	% below is a project-internal descent (finite sheaf equalizer over the cover + flatness of
3410	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is
3411	% gap1 itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3412	\begin{proof}
3413	  \uses{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent,
3414	    lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3415	    lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib, lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib,
3416	    lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
3417	  Refine the quasi-coherence cover to a finite basic-open cover \(\{D(r_j)\}_{j=1}^m\) of \(\Spec R\)
3418	  with each \(D(r_j) \le q.X_{i(j)}\)
3419	  (\cref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData}). On each \(D(r_j)\) the restricted
3420	  counit is an isomorphism (\cref{lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent}), so
3421	  \(M|_{D(r_j)} \cong \widetilde{N_j}\) with \(N_j = \Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) a module over \(R_{r_j}\),
3422	  and the basic-open sections of \(M\) below \(D(r_j)\) are the corresponding module localizations
3423	  (\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}).
3424	
3425	  Fix \(f \in R\). The sheaf condition for \(M\) over the finite cover \(\{D(r_j)\}\) and its pairwise
3426	  intersections presents the global sections as the finite equalizer
3427	  \[
3428	    \Gamma(M, \top) \;=\; \mathrm{eq}\Bigl(\textstyle\prod_j \Gamma(M, D(r_j))
3429	      \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{j,k} \Gamma(M, D(r_jr_k))\Bigr),
3430	  \]
3431	  the Mayer--Vietoris / unique-gluing description of sections
3432	  (\cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}, \cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}); the same holds
3433	  with \(\top\) replaced by \(D(f)\) and the cover by \(\{D(fr_j)\}\). Localizing at
3434	  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact and commutes with this finite limit because localization is flat
3435	  (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}). On each cover piece the localization of
3436	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r_j))\) away from \(f\) is \(\Gamma(M, D(fr_j)) = \Gamma(M|_{D(r_j)}, D(f))\), a
3437	  localization of the tilde \(\widetilde{N_j}\) computed by
3438	  \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}; the same on the overlaps. Hence the localized equalizer
3439	  computes \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\), and the comparison map
3440	  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an isomorphism: the section
3441	  restriction is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\). This is the
3442	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})\) of Stacks
3443	  \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} on \(X = \Spec R\), \(X_f = D(f)\), obtained directly from
3444	  local-tilde data without appeal to the global affine equivalence.
3445	\end{proof}

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)

This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.

The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
transport of the previous section:

1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).

Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
is project-local. -/

section BasicOpenPresentationDescent

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology

namespace Scheme.Modules

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- **Presentation of the geometric restriction of `M` to an open `W` of a cover member** (gap1, P1).

For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and *any* open
`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction
`(pullback W.ι).obj ((pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M)` of `M` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`,
then to `W`) admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.

It slices the *global* presentation `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` of
`N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on `Z` down to the slice `N.over W` (a single `Presentation.map` of the
over-functor — the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, no iterated-slice equivalence
needed since `PN` is global on the genuine scheme `Z`), then geometrizes via
`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local: feeds the affine descent of the gap1 keystone
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
noncomputable def presentationPullbackιRestrict (M : X.Modules)
    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) (W : (q.X i).toScheme.Opens) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)).Presentation :=
  overRestrictPresentation W ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)
    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u}
      (presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i)
      (SheafOfModules.pushforward (𝟙 ((q.X i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl))

end Scheme.Modules

end BasicOpenPresentationDescent

end AlgebraicGeometry

end Scheme.Modules

end SliceGeometricPresentation

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The canonical morphism `(pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R :=
  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit :
    ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ) =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) =
      unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ :=
  Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _

instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) := by
  rw [isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective]
  intro M
  rw [← ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).bijective.of_comp_iff',
    ← (unitHomEquiv _).bijective.of_comp_iff']
  convert (bijective_pushforwardSections φ M).comp (unitHomEquiv _).bijective
  ext f : 1
  dsimp
  rw [pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
    Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
    pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit]

/-- The pullback of a free sheaf of modules is a free sheaf of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjFreeIso (I : Type u) :
    (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I :=
  (asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫
    Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom {I : Type u} (i : I) :
    (pullback φ).map (ιFree i) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ ≫ ιFree i := by
  simp [pullbackObjFreeIso, ιFree]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality {I J : Type u} (f : I → J) :
    (pullback φ).map (freeMap f) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ J).hom =
      (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom ≫ freeMap f :=
  Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (pullback φ) _ _
    (isColimitFreeCofan (R := S) I)) _ _ (fun i ↦ by simp [← Functor.map_comp_assoc])

/-- The canonical isomorphism `freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor` for a
continuous map between ringed sites, when the underlying functor between the sites
is final. -/
noncomputable def freeFunctorCompPullbackIso : freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ pullbackObjFreeIso φ X)

end SheafOfModules

/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).

For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
of schemes is `Final`. This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `Presentation.map`
along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf :=
  asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))

end Scheme.Modules

end BasicOpenPresentationDescent

noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme} (φ : Y ≅ Z) : Opens ↥Y ≌ Opens ↥Z where
  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base

        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by

      Opens.mapId _
example {Y Z : Scheme} (φ : Y ≅ Z) : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by

/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).

For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
of schemes is `Final`. This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `Presentation.map`
along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf :=
  asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))
input.new_string:
/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
      Opens.mapId _

/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is final.** Immediate from
`opensMapEquivOfIso` (an equivalence is final); the `Final` instance needed by
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. Project-local. -/
instance opensMap_final_of_schemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by
  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence := (opensMapEquivOfIso φ).isEquivalence_functor
  infer_instance

  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base

      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
      Opens.mapId _

/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on every affine open of a cover member** (gap1, P1).

For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and an *affine* open
`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine
`Spec Γ((q.X i).toScheme, W) ≅ W` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, then to `W`, then across
the affine identification `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`) has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit — i.e. it is
a geometric tilde.

This is the geometric heart of the gap1 per-element transport: the slice presentation supplied by
the quasi-coherence datum geometrizes (`presentationPullbackιRestrict`) to a global presentation on
`W.toScheme`, which transports across the affine iso (`presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`) to a global
presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`; a global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an
isomorphism (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`). Project-local: Mathlib has no
`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge. -/
theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback (M : X.Modules)
    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I)
    (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) (hW : IsAffineOpen W) :
    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hW.isoSpec.inv).obj
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=
  isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation _
    (presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso hW.isoSpec
      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))
      (presentationPullbackιRestrict M q i W))

end Scheme.Modules

/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover** (gap1, P1 keystone,
`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`).

Let `M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` with quasi-coherence data `q`, and let `r : R` with
`D(r) ≤ q.X i` for some cover member. Then the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine basic open
`D(r)` — realised as the preimage `W := (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` inside the cover-member subscheme
`Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, transported across the affine identification `W ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)` (which is

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added: 7** (all axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):
  1. `presentationPullbackιRestrict` (line ~1167) — presentation of `(W.ι^* (q.X i).ι^* M)` for any
     open `W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme`.
  2. `opensMapEquivOfIso` (~1198) — `Opens.map φ.inv.base` is an equivalence of opens sites for an
     iso of schemes `φ`.
  3. `opensMap_final_of_schemeIso` (~1216) — that opens functor is `Final`.
  4. `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso` (~1227) — pullback along an iso of schemes sends `unit` to `unit`.
  5. `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso` (~1247) — transport a presentation across pullback by a
     scheme iso (the affine-identification step).
  6. `isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback` (~1268) — **general keystone**: M restricts to a tilde on
     EVERY affine open `W` of a cover member `q.X i`.
  7. `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (~1299) — **the pinned P1 keystone**
     `lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`: the basic-open `D(r) ≤ q.X i` instance of (6).
- **Declarations blocked: 0.**
- **Sorry count: 4 → 4** (unchanged; the 4 pre-existing protected stubs at lines 123/161/198/225).
  The keystone added ZERO sorries.

## isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen (line ~1299) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach (the planner's 5-step affine descent, Z-route):**
  1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* presentation of
     `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the genuine scheme `Z = (q.X i).toScheme`.
  2. Slice it to `N.over W` via the SIMPLE single `Presentation.map (pushforward (𝟙 …)) (by rfl)`
     (the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, Quasicoherent.lean:314) — **no iterated-slice
     equivalence needed** because `PN` is already a global presentation on the scheme `Z`. This is the
     decisive simplification vs. the D(r)-direct route (which would need the `bind` iterated slice).
  3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` geometrizes to a presentation of `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on
     `W.toScheme` (reused this iter's lemma). → `presentationPullbackιRestrict`.
  4. `W` affine (= preimage of `D(r)`); `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec : W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z,W)`; transport
     across this iso via `Presentation.map (pullback isoSpec.inv) (pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso)`. The
     unit-iso needs `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)`, which holds because the iso's `Opens.map` is
     `Final`. → `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`.
  5. `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` on the resulting global presentation on `Spec Γ(Z,W)`.
- Affineness of `W`: `IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r` (`D(r)` affine) +
  `IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (q.X i).ι` with `D(r) ≤ q.X i = (q.X i).ι.opensRange`
  (`Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι`).
- **Conclusion form:** `IsIso (fromTildeΓ …)` of `M` pulled back to `Spec Γ(↥(q.X i), W)`
  (`W = (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)`). Γ(↥(q.X i), W) ≅ R_r by `isLocalization_basicOpen`, so this IS
  "M|_{D(r)} as the tilde of Γ(M,D(r)) on Spec R_r ≅ D(r)", matching the blueprint statement.

  hits the SAME `Functor.Final (Opens.map j.base)` wall for an open immersion that PROGRESS flags as a
  do-not-retry; the iso-only `Final` (step 4) sidesteps it.

## Key non-obvious facts (for memory / next iter)
- **`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` for a scheme iso `φ`:** to get `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit
  φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)` you need BOTH `haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final` (build it from an
  `Equivalence` via `opensMapEquivOfIso` — instance resolution CANNOT invert `φ.inv.base`, so it must
  be a `haveI`, not an `instance`) AND `haveI : (pushforward …).IsRightAdjoint := inferInstance`
  (synthesis does not find it transitively).

  NOT `Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv`. Although defeq, the latter carries its own bundled

  synthesis see a syntactically-different (defeq) term and FAIL. The `SheafOfModules` form coerces

now resolves). The planner/reviewer should add blueprint blocks for:

- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback` — the general

  `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`. **This is strictly more general than the pinned basic-open keystone and is
  the form D should consume.**

The pinned `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` additionally uses `IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen`,
`IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion`, `Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι`.

## Summary

I built the **QUOT gap1 P1 keystone** `isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen` (the blueprint's `lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`) plus 6 supporting lemmas in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean` — **7 new declarations, all axiom-clean** (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`), **0 new sorries** (the 4 remaining are the pre-existing protected stubs).

The keystone follows the planner's 5-step affine descent (Z-route), which avoids the documented open-immersion-finality dead end:

1. `presentationPullbackιRestrict` — slices the *global* presentation of `M` pulled back to `Z = (q.X i).toScheme` down to any open `W ⊆ Z` (simple `Presentation.map` of the over-functor — no iterated slice needed since the presentation is already global on the genuine scheme `Z`).
2–5. `opensMapEquivOfIso` → `opensMap_final_of_schemeIso` → `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso` → `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso` transport the presentation across the affine identification `W ≅ Spec Γ(Z,W)` (the iso's `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso), and `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` concludes.

I also provided `isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback` — a strictly more general form (tilde on *every* affine open of a cover member) that is the right shape for the downstream keystone D.

Key non-obvious findings (recorded in memory and `task_results`): the scheme-iso unit-iso needs both an explicit `Final` *haveI* and an `IsRightAdjoint` *haveI*, and must be stated via `SheafOfModules.pullback (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)` rather than `Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv` (else `asIso`'s `IsIso` synthesis fails on a defeq-but-syntactically-different term).

Next iter (out of scope here): the gap1 keystone D `section_localization_descent` should consume the general `isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback` + the existing `exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData` and `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict`.
